strategy: Archetype-first design: classify engineering domain → resolve one technology profile → build archetype-aware IR → load exactly one design skill pack via resolver. Adapter emits stack-specific designContracts (OpenAPI, RAML, AsyncAPI).

archetype-technology-resolver.label: Archetype & Technology Resolver
archetype-technology-resolver.primaryGoal: Classify application archetype, resolve technology profile, emit executionContext with single loadedSkillPack.
bestPractices: Classify every upload by content before archetype resolution

core-ir.label: Core Design Model
core-ir.primaryGoal: Produce archetype-aware technology-independent IR per archetypeRules in schema.
bestPractices: apiOperations[] must be complete enough for downstream OpenAPI/RAML generation
messaging section complete when asyncapi contract applies

design-skill-adapter.label: Design Skill Adapter
design-skill-adapter.primaryGoal: Load one skill pack; map IR to stack artifacts including mandatory designContracts.
bestPractices: Generate every mandatory designContract from profile.designContracts
OpenAPI/RAML paths and operations must align with IR apiOperations
Emit optional asyncapi when IR messaging non-empty

design-deliverable.label: Design Deliverable
design-deliverable.primaryGoal: Two stakeholder files; include design contract summary and paths.
bestPractices: Include designContracts section with format, path, operation count
Flag missing mandatory contracts in meta.missingContracts[]

validation.label: Validation Design
validation.primaryGoal: JSON quality gate; CRITICAL on missing mandatory design contracts.
criticalChecks: Mandatory designContract files exist for profileId
Backend: Application/openapi.yaml matches IR apiOperations count
MuleSoft: Integration/integration-apis.raml present and valid RAML header
Frontend: Application/client-api.openapi.yaml when IR apiOperations non-empty
OpenAPI/RAML operations align with IR — no invented endpoints
meta.adapterStatus not missing when profile resolved
